'''Jordan Lane''' (born 20 October 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]]  player who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan at [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

==Background==
Lane was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], East Riding of Yorkshire, England.

==Career==
===Hull FC===
In 2018, he made his [[Super League]] début for Hull against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. In October 2017, Lane agreed to join [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] on loan for the 2018 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullfc.com/news/2017-10-19-quartet-join-doncaster-on-loan|title=Quartet join Doncaster on Loan|publisher=Hull F.C.|date=19 October 2017|accessdate=15 March 2018|archive-date=24 January 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180124135856/http://www.hullfc.com/news/2017-10-19-quartet-join-doncaster-on-loan|url-status=dead}}</ref>
In September 2019, Lane was awarded with Hull FC's Young Player of the Year award. Lane played 26 matches for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 15 September 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 4-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=15 Sep 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Hull FC confirm the exit of eight players at the end of 2025 Super League season|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-confirm-the-exit-of-eight-players-at-the-end-of-2025-super-league-season/}}</ref>
